key_insight: Robin-Neumann coupling treats PINN and FEM as trace-to-flux operators, yielding a provably contracting interface scheme even when the PINN is only an approximate Steklov operator.
engineering_relevance: This enables robust meshless–mesh-based coupling for fluid-structure interaction and contact problems, reducing remeshing needs while improving interface stability over standard Dirichlet-Neumann coupling.
method: Domain-decomposition view with Robin-Neumann interface conditions and closed-form impedance
PINN-specific contraction analysis using a perturbed Steklov-Poincaré operator
Fourier-mode interface probe to estimate resolvable Steklov eigenvalues and spectral cap
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that predicted PINN-FEM contraction rates match measurements within 7% on 1D/2D Poisson tests, eigenvalue probing is accurate to within 0.5%, and the contact reaction matches submerged weight to 0.4% under refinement.
watch_item: The warm-started PINN can drift off the Stokes manifold over long time horizons, and under-resolution may miss pre-impact squeeze-film effects.
paper_title: Robin-Neumann Coupling of PINN and FEM Solvers: A Steklov-Poincaré View, with Application to Fluid-Structure Interaction with Contact
